Nxb4 wins a free pawn and attacks the queen.

The a-pawn promotes on a8. The a8 square is a light square. The bishop on h7 is on a light square too - so it's the RIGHT color bishop (controls a8). This means it's winning for white in general.

- c6b7 - goes toward the corner, tries to block
- c6c7 - also heading to block

After c6b7, I can head to a8/a7 to block the pawn. The king needs to stop the pawn from promoting.

If c6b7, white plays a6+ (pawn pushes? No, pawn is on a5, goes to a6). Then Kb7 blocks... actually after Kb7, white could play Kb5, a6+ Ka7, and then try to push.

Actually, with correct bishop color, white wins. But I should still play the most resilient move. c6b7 heads to the corner to block the pawn.

- Can white capture? Qe3xe4 takes. Then I lose the knight for... nothing immediate.
- Wait, but after Qxe4, my bishop on g4 attacks the queen! Bxe4... no wait, bishop on g4 can't reach e4 (different color squares - g4 is light, e4 is light actually). g4 is light square, e4 is light square. Yes Bxe4 wins the queen!

Wait let me verify: g4 - g is 7th file, 4th rank. (7+4)=11 odd = light. e4: (5+4)=9 odd = light. Same color! Bxe4 works.

So: Ne4+ Qxe4 Bxe4 - I win the queen for a knight!

But wait, does Qxe4 have to happen? Other options after Ne4+:
- Kc3-b2, Kc3-d3 (d3 attacked by Ne4? No, knight on e4 attacks d2, f2, c3, g3, c5, g5, d6, f6. Not d3). Kd3 is possible but Bxe4+ still wins queen... wait no, if king moves, queen doesn't move.

Actually the cleanest: after Ne4+, white must address check. If Kb2 or Kd3, I've at minimum harassed and can play Nxe3 winning queen? No wait, knight on e4 after king moves - Nxe3? Hmm no, the queen is on e3 and knight is on e4, knight doesn't attack e3 (adjacent square, knight doesn't move one square).

Let me reconsider. After Ne4+:
- Qxe4 Bxe4 wins queen for knight ✓
- Kb2: knight safe, I'm up tempo
- Kd3: knight attacks king? No king on d3, knight on e4, distance is 1 diagonal - not knight attack. But Kd3 is attacked by my bishop? No. Knight still attacked by... nothing. So Kd3 is legal but then Bf5 pins something or c5 threats.

This looks strong. Let me play Ne4+.

exd3 is simply winning a bishop.

After Kg2 - wait king can't go g2 (attacked). After Ke2, I play Kd4 approaching. Then Kf2 or Ke1 etc.
